title: The Earth's Tipping Point on Natural Resources
subtitle: 联合国最新研究：自然资源破坏已超地球承受极限

paragraphs.en: Degradation of the world's natural resources by humans is rapidly outpacing the planet's ability to absorb the damage, a recent UN environmental study has found. The study concludes that without radical action the level of prosperity that millions of people in the developed world count on will be impossible to maintain or extend to poorer countries.
paragraphs.zh: 联合国近期一项环境研究发现，人类对全球自然资源的破坏速度正迅速超过地球吸收这些损害的能力。研究结论指出，若不采取根本性行动，发达国家数百万人所依赖的繁荣水平将无法维持，更难以惠及较贫困国家。

paragraphs.en: Water scarcity is the curse of some of the poorest regions on Earth, leaving developing countries increasingly unable to feed themselves, and causing hardship for millions of people. There appears little prospect of this desperate situation being remedied without radical action being taken.
paragraphs.zh: 水资源短缺是地球上部分最贫困地区的祸根，导致发展中国家越来越难以实现粮食自给，给数百万人带来深重苦难。如果不采取根本性行动，这一严峻局面几乎没有改善的希望。

paragraphs.en: Water sources are under increasing threat from population growth, climate change, rapid urbanisation, rising levels of consumption, and the degradation of lands that previously provided a natural replenishment of water resources. The rate of damage to the natural environment was found to increase globally, despite concerted efforts to persuade governments to take measures to improve the condition.
paragraphs.zh: 水源正受到人口增长、气候变化、快速城市化、消费水平上升以及曾能自然补充水资源的土地退化等因素日益严重的威胁。研究发现，尽管各方协力劝说各国政府采取措施改善环境状况，全球自然环境受损的速度仍在加快。

paragraphs.en: "If current trends continue, and the world fails to improve patterns of production and consumption, then the state of the world's environment will continue to decline," warned UN executive director Achim Steiner. He said the tools for improving the environment for millions of people existed in developed countries, but were in danger of not being used.
paragraphs.zh: 联合国执行主任阿希姆·施泰纳警告说：“如果当前趋势持续下去，且全球未能改善生产与消费模式，那么世界环境状况将持续恶化。”他指出，发达国家其实拥有改善数百万人环境的工具，但这些工具有可能被闲置不用。

paragraphs.en: The study found that basic measures to tackle some of the key causes of environmental damage were still not being taken. These included measures to reduce air pollution; to control the damage to marine ecosystems, which can have a huge effect on fish stocks on which hundreds of millions of people depend; and to curb the degradation of land where modern agricultural methods were pursued without regard to the longer-term consequences.
paragraphs.zh: 研究发现，针对环境破坏部分关键成因的基本治理措施仍未得到落实。这些措施包括减少空气污染、控制对海洋生态系统的破坏（这关乎数亿人赖以生存的鱼类资源），以及遏制不顾长远后果盲目推行现代农业方式所造成的土地退化。

paragraphs.en: Despite the recent global agreement on cutting greenhouse gas emissions, global carbon output continues to rise. This will put a long-term strain on the ability of developing economies to feed their own people. Climate change is aggravated by the emissions of greenhouse gases from chemical and natural fertilisers used in agriculture which increased by more than a quarter between 2000 and 2010.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管近期全球达成了减少温室气体排放的协议，但全球碳排放量仍在持续攀升。这将给发展中经济体养活本国人口的能力带来长期压力。农业生产中使用的化肥和天然肥料所排放的温室气体在2000年至2010年间增加了超过四分之一，进一步加剧了气候变化。

paragraphs.en: Other problem areas identified in the report included glaciers, which provide vital water resources for millions of people, but which are shrinking as the climate warms. In rich countries, these problems have built up over decades and centuries while economic growth was pursued at the expense of the environment. Subsequent efforts to remedy the environment have met with partial success.
paragraphs.zh: 报告中指出的其他问题领域还包括冰川。冰川为数百万人提供着至关重要的水资源，却因气候变暖而日益萎缩。在富裕国家，这些问题是几十乃至几百年来以牺牲环境为代价追求经济增长所累积而成的，此后为治理环境所做的努力仅取得了部分成效。

paragraphs.en: But in developing countries, the path of future development has more potential to change, which has encouraged international institutions to devise more sustainable growth pathways that are supposed both to alleviate poverty and preserve the environment.
paragraphs.zh: 但在发展中国家，未来的发展道路则蕴含更大的变革潜力。这促使国际机构着手设计更可持续的增长路径，以期既能减轻贫困，又能保护环境。
